title: FxPro Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: FxPro Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). FxPro (4.2★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using FxPro — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
## What is FxPro?

FxPro is a regulated forex and CFD broker founded in 2006 that specializes in low-latency, NDD (No Dealing Desk) execution across four distinct trading platforms: MT4, MT5, cTrader, and its proprietary FxPro Edge platform. With a 4.2/5 rating and headquarters in London, FxPro serves over 2 million traders globally across 150+ countries, offering access to 2,100+ instruments spanning forex, indices, commodities, stocks, and cryptocurrencies. The broker is regulated by top-tier authorities including the FCA (UK), CySEC (Cyprus), and FSCA (South Africa), making it a solid choice for traders seeking institutional-grade execution without the high minimums. FxPro is best suited for forex traders, algorithmic traders, and those who prefer the cTrader platform or need serious backtesting and automated trading capabilities—and all platforms offer free demo and live accounts to get started.

## How to Create Your FxPro Account

Creating an FxPro account takes about 5-10 minutes and requires minimal information upfront:

  • Visit the FxPro registration page. Go to FxPro.com and click "Open Account" in the top navigation. You'll be directed to a simple registration form.
  • Enter your personal details. Provide your full name, email address, phone number, and country of residence. FxPro will ask what type of trader you are (retail, professional, or institutional) to determine which regulations apply to your account.
  • Set your password. Create a strong password—FxPro requires at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and at least one number or special character.
  • Verify your email. Check your inbox for a verification email from FxPro and click the confirmation link within 24 hours.
  • Complete identity verification (KYC). FxPro will ask for a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID) and proof of address (utility bill or bank statement from the last 3 months). Upload these documents via their secure portal.
  • Complete your profile. Answer questions about your trading experience, investment knowledge, and financial situation. This is mandatory under regulatory requirements and determines whether you qualify for professional trader status (which allows higher leverage).
  • Choose your platform and account type. Select from MT4 Standard, MT5 Standard, cTrader Raw (zero commission, raw spreads from 0.0 pips), or FxPro Edge. Start with a demo account to test the platform before risking real capital.
  • Fund your account (optional for demo). If you move to a live account, FxPro accepts deposits via card, bank transfer, e-wallets (Skrill, Neteller, PayPal), and cryptocurrency. Minimum deposit varies by payment method but typically starts at $100 USD equivalent.

Verification usually completes within 24 hours on business days. Once approved, you can immediately access your demo account with virtual capital to practice.

## Setting Up FxPro for the First Time

After logging into your FxPro account for the first time, you'll land on the Client Portal dashboard. Here's how to configure it properly:

  • Download your chosen platform. If you selected MT4 or MT5, download the installer from the "Trading Platforms" section and install it on your desktop or laptop. For cTrader, a web-based version is available directly in your browser, though the desktop app offers faster execution. FxPro Edge has its own dedicated installer.
  • Log in to your platform. Use your FxPro account login credentials. Your live account number will be formatted as a six-digit account ID (e.g., 123456). Make sure you're connecting to the correct server—FxPro typically uses "FxPro-MT4" or "FxPro-MT5" in the server dropdown.
  • Configure your market watch. Add the instruments you plan to trade to your Market Watch window. Start with major pairs (EUR/USD, GBP/USD) and add indices or commodities once you're comfortable. Right-click and select "Show/Hide" to customize which symbols appear.
  • Set up alerts. In MT4/MT5, go to Tools → Options and configure push notifications for your mobile app (if you want alerts on your phone). FxPro's mobile app supports both iOS and Android and mirrors your live account data in real-time.
  • Review account settings in the Client Portal. Log into your account at MyFxPro and verify your leverage setting (defaults to 1:100 for EU residents, up to 1:500 for non-EU traders), commission structure for cTrader accounts, and spread settings for your account type.
  • Test your connection with a demo trade. Place a small test order in your demo account to ensure everything is working. This confirms your platform connection, spreads are loading correctly, and order execution feels responsive.
  • Enable two-factor authentication (2FA). In your Client Portal settings, enable 2FA using an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or Microsoft Authenticator. This protects your account from unauthorized access.

## Essential Features You Should Know

  • NDD Execution with Raw Spreads on cTrader: FxPro's cTrader Raw accounts offer zero-commission trading with spreads from 0.0 pips on major pairs. This means you pay only the actual interbank spread plus a fixed commission per side (typically $3.50 per million traded). For scalpers and high-frequency traders, this is significantly cheaper than standard account spreads of 1.5+ pips. Access your cTrader account through the web platform or download the desktop client.
  • Backtesting and Strategy Tester: MT4 and MT5 include built-in strategy testers where you can backtest Expert Advisors on historical data dating back 15+ years. Open MetaEditor (F11 in MT4/MT5), code or import your EA, then use the Strategy Tester (Ctrl+R) to run tests on 1-minute to monthly bars. FxPro provides clean tick data, so your backtest results are reliable.
  • Mobile Trading App: The FxPro mobile app for iOS and Android synchronizes with your live account and allows you to open, modify, and close positions from anywhere. Set pending orders, manage risk with stop losses, and receive push notifications when your alerts trigger. The app includes live charts, economic calendars, and news feeds, so you don't need to check your desktop constantly.
  • API Access for Algorithmic Trading: Both MT4 and MT5 support Expert Advisors (EAs) and cTrader supports cAlgo scripts, allowing you to automate your trading strategies. FxPro's servers are optimized for low-latency EA execution, and you can run multiple EAs on a single account. For more advanced automation, cTrader's C# API gives developers full programmatic control over order placement and account management.
  • Performance Analytics and Trade Journal: MT5 includes built-in analytics showing your Sharpe ratio, recovery factor, and maximum drawdown. For detailed trade journaling, use the "History" tab to export all closed trades as a CSV and analyze your win rate, average profit/loss per trade, and seasonal patterns. This data is essential for improving your edge over time.
  • Economic Calendar and News Feed: Access FxPro's integrated economic calendar showing upcoming central bank announcements, inflation prints, and employment data. The calendar includes volatility forecasts so you know which events typically move the markets you trade. The in-platform news feed aggregates headlines from major financial outlets.
  • Risk Management Tools: All FxPro platforms include stop loss and take profit orders, guaranteed stop losses (available for an additional cost on volatile instruments), trailing stops, and One-Cancels-Other (OCO) orders. On MT4/MT5, you can set maximum daily loss limits to auto-stop trading if you hit a loss threshold for the day.

## FxPro Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

FxPro's pricing model is unique because all four platforms are completely free to use—there's no platform fee or subscription cost. Here's what you're actually paying for:

  • Demo Account: $0/month. Start here if you're new to trading or FxPro. You get virtual capital (typically $100,000 USD equivalent) to practice with real market prices and no real-money risk. Demo accounts are permanent and don't expire, so you can use them as long as needed.
  • MT4 / MT5 Standard: $0/month. Live trading on FxPro's most popular platforms with fixed spreads (1.5+ pips on EUR/USD, wider on exotics). Best for beginners and retail traders who prioritize simplicity over ultra-tight spreads. Minimum deposit is typically $100, and leverage goes up to 1:500 for non-EU traders.
  • cTrader Raw: $0/month. Zero commission on spreads, but pay a per-trade commission ($3.50 per million traded). Raw spreads start at 0.0 pips, so your total cost depends on interbank pricing. Best for scalpers, high-frequency traders, and anyone trading 50+ lots per month. Same $100 minimum deposit and up to 1:500 leverage.
  • FxPro Edge: $0/month. FxPro's proprietary platform with a modern interface, built-in copy trading, and advanced charting. No spreads or commissions listed separately, but pricing is similar to cTrader Raw for competitive accounts. Edge is designed for traders who want a more intuitive interface than MT4/MT5.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners should start with MT4 Standard for its simplicity and wide broker support (you can easily migrate EAs between brokers). Intermediate traders who scalp or trade frequently should move to cTrader Raw to reduce costs. Advanced algorithmic traders benefit from MT5's superior backtesting and API capabilities, combined with cTrader Raw for execution. All traders should start on a demo account to test their setup before risking capital.

Important caveat: FxPro charges an inactivity fee of $10/month after 6 months with zero trading activity. If you open an account but don't trade, make at least one small trade every 6 months to avoid this fee.

## Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of FxPro

  • Use cTrader's DOM (Depth of Market) for better order placement. cTrader's DOM shows bid/ask queues and lets you click directly on price levels to enter orders, much like a crypto exchange. If you trade illiquid pairs or large sizes, this prevents slippage compared to MT4's market orders.
  • Backtest with FxPro's tick data, not M1 bars. Go to Tools → History Center in MT4/MT5, download tick data for your symbol, then backtest using "Every tick" mode instead of "Open prices only." This simulates real slippage and stop loss fills accurately.
  • Export your trading data monthly for tax and performance tracking. In your Client Portal, go to History → Statements and download CSV statements for your accountant and personal records. Many traders miss deductions because they don't organize this data.
  • Combine FxPro with TradingView for charting. FxPro doesn't have the most advanced charting, so many pros use MT4/MT5 for execution but analyze charts in TradingView (which has better Pine Script capabilities). Connect TradingView alerts to your FxPro account via webhooks or email for trade signals.
  • Set up separate demo and live accounts with different leverage settings. Use your demo account with 1:500 leverage to test risky strategies, then trade your live account at 1:100 or 1:50 for capital preservation. This way you can experiment safely.
  • Monitor your account risk ratio daily. Calculate your account risk as (daily loss limit) ÷ (account balance) × 100. For example, if you have $10,000 and lose $200, that's 2% daily risk. Keep this under 3% unless you're a professional trader with a proven strategy.
  • Use FxPro's VPS partnership for 24/5 automated trading without shutting down your laptop. FxPro partners with VPS providers to host your MT4/MT5 and EAs on remote servers. This ensures your EAs run 24/5 without latency from your home internet connection.

## Common FxPro Issues and How to Fix Them

  • Issue: "Connection to server failed" or platform won't log in. Solution: Check that you're connecting to the correct FxPro server (look at the server dropdown in MT4/MT5 login—it should say "FxPro-MT4" or similar). Verify your account number and password are correct. If still stuck, log into your Client Portal to confirm your account is active and has no pending verification. Restart your platform and try again. If the problem persists, contact FxPro support; server maintenance occasionally requires restarting the connection.
  • Issue: "ERR 130: Invalid stops" when trying to place an order. Solution: This usually means your stop loss is too close to your entry price. FxPro has minimum stop distance requirements (typically 5 pips for major pairs, more for exotics). Move your stop loss further away from entry, then resubmit the order.
  • Issue: High spreads or slippage on live trading vs. demo. Solution: Demo accounts sometimes show tighter spreads than live accounts because there's no real capital at risk. Confirm you're trading during normal market hours (London or US sessions for forex); spreads widen during Asian hours and on weekends. If you're on a Standard account, consider switching to cTrader Raw for more consistent tight spreads. Check that your account isn't using aggressive leverage or trading illiquid pairs, which incur wider spreads.
  • Issue: Unable to withdraw funds after opening an account. Solution: FxPro requires a withdrawal be made to the same payment method you deposited from (anti-money laundering rule). If you deposited via card, withdraw to that same card first. After one successful withdrawal, you can withdraw to other methods. Processing times: cards typically 3-5 business days, bank transfers 5-7 days, e-wallets 24 hours.

## Is FxPro Worth It? Our Verdict

FxPro earns its 4.2/5 rating as a solid, regulated choice for serious forex and CFD traders. With four platforms to choose from, zero platform fees, and raw spreads from 0.0 pips on cTrader, it delivers institutional-quality execution at retail prices. The 20-year track record, FCA regulation, and support for automated trading make it reliable for long-term traders. However, it's not ideal for US traders (completely unavailable), and the 6-month inactivity fee is annoying if you trade sporadically. If you trade forex, indices, or commodities and want low costs with professional tools, FxPro is worth trying. For a comparison, see how FxPro compares to OANDA or FxPro vs. Interactive Brokers. Start with a demo account today—it takes 10 minutes and commits you to nothing.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
